_comment: PoE Expedition remnant keyword reference. Organized by effect category.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters are Hexproof
remnants.effect: Monsters cannot be cursed. All curse-based damage and utility (Vulnerability, Temporal Chains, etc.) are completely negated.
remnants.notes: Hard counter to curse builds. Skip unless you deal non-curse damage.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters deal 50% more Damage
remnants.effect: All damage from Expedition monsters is multiplied by 1.5×. Affects all damage types.
remnants.notes: One-shots at moderate character investment. Avoid unless very tanky.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters have 50% more Maximum Life
remnants.effect: Monsters have 1.5× their normal life pool. Significantly increases kill time.
remnants.notes: Annoying but rarely lethal. Combined with Regeneration becomes a real problem.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters Regenerate 5% of Life per second
remnants.effect: Monsters heal 5% of their max life every second. Sustained DPS required to out-damage healing.
remnants.notes: Paired with increased life this can make monsters near-unkillable. Avoid if DPS is limited.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters deal 30% more Physical Damage
remnants.effect: Physical damage from monsters is 1.3× normal. Increases bleed/stun risk.
remnants.notes: Problematic against squishy builds. Physical reduction and armour help.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters deal 25% more Elemental Damage
remnants.effect: All elemental damage (fire, cold, lightning) is 1.25× normal.
remnants.notes: Applies to all three elements at once. Elemental resistance capping is essential.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters have 30% increased Attack Speed
remnants.effect: Monsters attack 30% faster. Greatly increases incoming hit frequency.
remnants.notes: Especially dangerous against melee players. Combined with damage modifiers, can be lethal.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters have 30% increased Cast Speed
remnants.effect: Spellcasting Expedition monsters cast 30% faster. More spell projectiles per second.
remnants.notes: Primarily impacts projectile-spewing caster monsters.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters have Onslaught
remnants.effect: Monsters gain the Onslaught buff: 20% increased attack/cast speed and 20% increased movement speed.
remnants.notes: Effectively stacks on top of other speed modifiers. Combined with damage mods, very lethal.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters deal 30% more Chaos Damage
remnants.effect: Chaos damage is 1.3× normal. Ignores energy shield.
remnants.notes: Bypasses ES entirely. Full chaos resistance capping (75%) is mandatory here.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters cannot be Slowed
remnants.effect: Chill, temporal chains, and any slow effects do not reduce monster speed.
remnants.notes: Negates cold-based kiting strategies. Movement speed matters more.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters have Elemental Ailment Immunity
remnants.effect: Monsters cannot be Ignited, Chilled, Frozen, or Shocked.
remnants.notes: Disables ailment-scaling builds (Ignite, Freeze). DPS from hits unaffected.

remnants.keyword: All Runic Monsters are Enraged
remnants.effect: Monsters gain the Enraged buff, dealing significantly more damage.
remnants.notes: One of the most dangerous generic damage modifiers. Hard to offset without high EHP.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters fully Regenerate Life if not hit recently
remnants.effect: Monsters fully heal in a brief window between hits. Sustained DPS is mandatory.
remnants.notes: Demands continuous damage output. DoT builds handle this naturally; hit builds need high hit rate.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters Steal Power, Frenzy, and Endurance Charges on Hit
remnants.effect: Each monster hit removes a charge from the player and adds it to the monster.
remnants.notes: Build-breaking against charge-dependent characters. Avoid if your build relies on endurance, frenzy, or power charges.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters are Possessed
remnants.effect: Monsters are inhabited by Tormented Spirits, gaining extra modifiers and emitting auras.
remnants.notes: Unpredictable — spirit modifiers vary. Generally increases difficulty.

remnants.keyword: Area contains additional Runic Monster Markers
remnants.effect: More Expedition monster packs spawn in the detonation area. More packs = more loot.
remnants.notes: Always include. More markers is almost always better for loot density.

remnants.keyword: Remnant has 25% increased Item Quantity
remnants.effect: Items dropped from Runic Monsters in range are 25% more numerous.
remnants.notes: Standard loot multiplier. Always worth including.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters drop additional Expedition Artifacts
remnants.effect: Each Runic Monster drop has a chance to include extra Expedition artifacts (Astragali, Exotic Coinage, etc.).
remnants.notes: Expedition artifacts are the primary Expedition currency. Strong include.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters drop additional Expedition Logbooks
remnants.effect: Increases the chance and quantity of Expedition Logbooks dropped by Runic Monsters.
remnants.notes: Logbooks are valuable — always include if you want to run more logbooks.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters drop additional Currency Items
remnants.effect: Runic Monsters have a chance to drop extra currency items with each kill.
remnants.notes: Reliable chaos/exalt source. One of the best Expedition modifiers.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters drop additional Rare or Unique Items
remnants.effect: Runic Monster kills include extra rare or unique item drops.
remnants.notes: High value for loot quantity builds. Excellent modifier.

remnants.keyword: Expedition contains additional Runic Treasure Chests
remnants.effect: Extra Runic Treasure Chests (requiring a specific key to open) appear in the area.
remnants.notes: Value depends on which keys you have. Generally good if you have chest keys stockpiled.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters have 15% increased Item Rarity
remnants.effect: Items dropped by Runic Monsters have 15% higher rarity bonus.
remnants.notes: Good but weaker than IIQ. Include if available.

remnants.keyword: Area contains 25% increased number of Expedition Remnants
remnants.effect: More Remnant runic stones appear in the detonation area, giving more modifier choices.
remnants.notes: More remnants = more control over encounter difficulty and loot. Always good.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters have 75% chance to drop a Rare item
remnants.effect: Greatly increases the proportion of Runic Monster drops that are rare (yellow) items.
remnants.notes: Excellent for self-found players. Less impactful if you're filtering for high-value items only.

remnants.keyword: Runic Monsters are Resurrected on Death
remnants.effect: Runic Monsters revive once after being killed. Must be defeated twice.
remnants.notes: Doubles effective monster life in practice. Slow if you don't have rapid burst damage.

remnants.keyword: Players are cursed with Temporal Chains
remnants.effect: Players' action speed is slowed. Affects movement, attack, cast, and cooldown speeds.
remnants.notes: One of the hardest debuffs — slows everything you do. Avoid unless you have Temporal Chains immunity.

remnants.keyword: Players deal 20% less damage
remnants.effect: All player damage is reduced by 20%. Slows kills and extends exposure to danger.
remnants.notes: Stacks poorly with other damage-reduction modifiers. Skip if already taking damage nerfs.

remnants.keyword: Players have 30% reduced Effect of Flasks
remnants.effect: Life, mana, and utility flasks are 30% less effective during this Expedition.
remnants.notes: Can be lethal for flask-reliant builds during tanky monster combos. Less impactful for ES builds.

remnants.keyword: Area contains an additional Runic Boss
remnants.effect: An additional named Expedition boss spawns in the detonation area.
remnants.notes: Bosses drop better loot but are significantly more dangerous than regular Runic Monsters.

remnants.keyword: Expedition has 50% increased Monster Pack Size
remnants.effect: Each Runic Monster pack is 50% larger in terms of individual monsters per pack.
remnants.notes: More monsters per pack = more loot but also more incoming damage. Worth it for most builds.
